stories.title: Add and Manage 'Replacing' and 'Is Replaced By' Relationships on Requirements in Backoffice
stories.as_a: requirements library manager working in the Backoffice
stories.i_want: to specify which requirements a new or edited requirement replaces (via a new multi-select field 'Replacing') and which requirements replace an existing one (via a new multi-select field 'Is Replaced By')
stories.so_that: the system can track requirement lineage, automatically set the status of replaced requirements to Inactive, and surface these changes in impacted releases
acceptance_criteria: The 'Add Requirement' and 'Edit Requirement' screens each include a new multi-select field labelled 'Replacing'. Clicking this field opens a searchable dropdown listing all requirements and sub-requirements in the system whose status is either 'Active' or 'Inactive'. The user can select and add multiple entries; each selected entry is displayed as a tag in the field.

The 'Edit Requirement' screen also includes a new multi-select field labelled 'Is Replaced By'. The same searchable dropdown applies. This field cannot be directly edited by the user via the Edit screen — it is populated and managed by the system when another requirement's 'Replacing' field references this requirement.

When a requirement has any value in its 'Is Replaced By' field, all of its editable fields except the 'Replacing' field become read-only and cannot be modified. An explanatory inline note must be shown: 'This requirement has been replaced. Remove the replacement to re-enable editing.' The 'Replacing' field remains editable so the user can add new replacement relationships.

When the user saves a requirement (new or edited) that has a non-empty 'Replacing' field: (1) A version-changing pop-up is shown asking when this change should be pushed to releases — this change is always classified as 'Major', even if all other changed fields would individually qualify as 'Minor'. (2) For each requirement/sub-requirement listed in 'Replacing', the system creates a new version with status set to 'Inactive' and appends the current requirement to that version's 'Is Replaced By' field. If 'Is Replaced By' already has values, the current requirement is added to the existing list — it does not overwrite existing entries.

When the user saves a change to a requirement by populating its 'Is Replaced By' field directly: (1) A version-changing pop-up is shown (Major change). (2) A new version of the current requirement is created with status 'Inactive'. (3) For each requirement listed in 'Is Replaced By', the current requirement is appended to that requirement's 'Replacing' field.

A requirement that already has a non-empty 'Is Replaced By' field cannot be selected as a target in another requirement's 'Replacing' field until the replacement relationship is first removed. If such a requirement appears in the searchable dropdown, it must be visually marked as unavailable for selection (e.g., greyed out or excluded) with a tooltip: 'Already replaced — remove existing replacement first.'

Both the new version of the replacing requirement and the new version of the replaced requirement (with its 'Is Replaced By' update) are classified as Major changes in the release. The version-change pop-up is shown for both.

If — while saving a requirement with 'Replacing' changes — any other fields are also modified (regardless of whether those fields are individually Minor), the entire save is still recorded as a 'Major' version. There is no scenario where modifying the 'Replacing' field results in a Minor version.
The 'Add Requirement' screen supports the 'Replacing' field for both parent requirements and sub-requirements.
stories.source_requirement: Backoffice section: 'Updating from Add Requirement and Edit Requirement screens with field Replacing' and 'Updating from Add Requirement and Edit Requirement screens with field Is Replaced By'. Notes: 'when the requirement has some value for the Is Replaced By field - it can no longer be updated until the replacement is removed' and 'if this field is not empty, the current requirement should be added to the existing list (instead of overwriting the value already specified).'

stories.title: Version and Status Management When Replacement Relationships on Existing Requirements Change
stories.as_a: requirements library manager updating an existing requirement that already has values in its 'Replacing' field
stories.i_want: the system to correctly classify the change type (Major or Minor) and update all affected requirements when I add, remove, or leave unchanged the replacement relationships
stories.so_that: version history is accurate and releases only receive notifications proportional to the significance of the change
acceptance_criteria: When only Minor fields are updated on a requirement that has a non-empty 'Replacing' field (and the 'Replacing' field itself is unchanged): the change is classified as 'Minor'. No version-change pop-up is shown on save. The new Minor version is added to the last version of each requirement referenced in 'Replacing' and appears in their 'Is Replaced By' field.

When at least one Major field is updated on a requirement with a non-empty 'Replacing' field (but 'Replacing' values are unchanged): the change is classified as 'Major'. The version-change pop-up is shown. The new Major version is propagated to the last version of each requirement in 'Replacing' and shown in their 'Is Replaced By' field.

When a new requirement/sub-requirement is added to the 'Replacing' field of a requirement that already contains other 'Replacing' values: the change is classified as 'Major'. The version-change pop-up is shown. For each newly added requirement: a new version is created with status 'Inactive' and the current requirement is appended to their 'Is Replaced By' field without overwriting existing entries.

When an existing requirement/sub-requirement is removed from the 'Replacing' field (but at least one other entry remains): the change is classified as 'Major'. The version-change pop-up is shown. For each removed requirement: a new version is created with status 'Inactive' and all previous versions of the current requirement are removed from that removed requirement's 'Is Replaced By' field.

When ALL requirements are removed from the 'Replacing' field (field becomes empty): the change is classified as 'Major'. The version-change pop-up is shown. For each removed requirement: a new version is created with status 'Inactive' and all previous versions of the current requirement are removed from their 'Is Replaced By' field.

After all replacements are removed, the system checks whether there exists a previous version of the current requirement that had an empty 'Replacing' field. If no such version exists: the resulting change is 'Major'. If such a version exists and the differences between that version and the current one involve only Minor fields (or no fields): the resulting change is 'Minor'. If such a version exists and the differences involve at least one Major field: the resulting change is 'Major'.

In all cases where a removal of a 'Replacing' entry creates a new version for the removed replacement requirement, only the newly created versions are given 'Inactive' status — prior versions of those requirements are not retroactively modified.
stories.source_requirement: Backoffice section: 'Updating requirements that were replacing other requirements' — covering all four sub-cases: Minor-only update, Major update, adding new entry to Replacing, removing partial entries, removing all entries.

stories.title: Export and Import Requirements with Replacement Data via File
stories.as_a: requirements library manager exporting or importing product and process requirements via file
stories.i_want: the 'Replacing' and 'Is Replaced By' columns to appear in export files, and 'Replacing' to be an optional column in import template files with validated processing
stories.so_that: requirement replacement relationships can be viewed in bulk exports and established via file upload without requiring individual UI edits
acceptance_criteria: The Product Requirements export file includes two new columns: 'Replacing' (comma-separated list of requirement codes replaced by this requirement) and 'Is Replaced By' (comma-separated list of requirement codes replacing this requirement). Both columns are positioned after the 'Is Active' column and before the 'Add Change on Date' column.

The Process Requirements export file includes the same two new columns — 'Replacing' and 'Is Replaced By' — with the same comma-separated format, positioned after 'Is Active' and before 'Add Change on Date'.

The Product Requirements import template file includes a new optional column 'Replacing'. This column accepts one or more requirement/sub-requirement codes separated by commas. It appears after 'Is Active' and before 'Add Change on Date'. There is no 'Is Replaced By' column in the template — this field is set by the system.
The Process Requirements import template file includes the same optional 'Replacing' column in the same format and position.

Upon uploading a completed template file, the system validates the 'Replacing' column for each row. If any code in the 'Replacing' field does not correspond to an existing requirement/sub-requirement in the system, or if multiple codes are separated by any symbol other than a comma, the import fails for that row with the error: "Row <row number> - 'Requirement specified for \"Replacing\" column does not exist in the system. Several codes can be separated only by comma.'" All other valid rows may still be processed.

When the 'Replacing' column passes validation for a row: for each requirement listed, the system creates a new version of that requirement with status 'Inactive' and appends the uploading requirement to its 'Is Replaced By' field. Existing 'Is Replaced By' entries are not overwritten.
If a requirement being uploaded has a non-empty 'Replacing' column and also has other field changes that would individually be Minor, the entire version is still saved as 'Major'.

A requirement that already has a non-empty 'Is Replaced By' value in the system cannot be updated via file upload until the replacement is removed. Rows in the import file that attempt to update such a requirement must return a validation error: "Row <row number> - This requirement has been replaced and cannot be updated via file upload. Remove the replacement first."
The export files correctly render comma-separated lists for requirements with multiple 'Replacing' or 'Is Replaced By' relationships (e.g., 'REQ-001,REQ-002,REQ-003').
stories.source_requirement: Backoffice section: 'Adding replaced requirements data for requirements with the file upload' — including export column lists for Product and Process Requirements, template column lists, validation rule table, and post-validation update behavior.

stories.title: View Requirements Update Warning and Requirements Update Pop-up in a Release
stories.as_a: release manager with a release in Creation & Scoping, Review & Confirm, or Manage stage
stories.i_want: to see a warning message when newer versions of my scoped requirements are available and to open a 'Requirements Update' pop-up that shows all applicable changes in a structured, filterable table
stories.so_that: I am always informed of pending requirement version changes and can review their nature before deciding whether to apply them
acceptance_criteria: When a release is in the 'Creation & Scoping', 'Review & Confirm', or 'Manage' stage and there are newer versions of requirements that are scoped for the release (or that could be scoped for the release) available in the system, a warning message is shown on the release.

acceptance_criteria.text: The warning message displayed depends on the release type and questionnaire state: (1) For a release created from scratch or cloned in 'Review & Confirm' or 'Manage' stage where the Questionnaire has no unanswered questions — message: 'New version of requirements is available. Please review update statuses if needed. Learn More' — where 'Learn More' is a clickable link. (2) For the same stages where unanswered questions exist on the Questionnaire tab — message: 'A new version of requirements is available. Please review updated Questionnaire and review Requirements Update' — where 'Questionnaire' is a clickable link opening the Questionnaire tab in the same window, and 'Requirements Update' is a separate clickable link. Cloned releases in Creation & Scoping stage follow the same patterns as above.

acceptance_criteria: Clicking the 'Learn More' link or the 'Requirements Update' link opens the 'Requirements Update' pop-up.

acceptance_criteria.text: The 'Requirements Update' pop-up displays: (1) A clickable 'Link to the Content Changes' at the top. (2) Sub-header: 'Review the proposed changes to requirements. Please select which changes to apply.' (3) A warning message 'Certain requirements will be updated automatically on the specified date.' — shown only when at least one requirement in the list has a 'Change on date' set AND the release is in Scoping or Scope Approval status.

acceptance_criteria: The pop-up contains filter controls: a Search field (filtering the list by requirement code, name, or description), a 'Change' dropdown filter (options: Updated, Replaced, Added, Removed), and a 'Reset' button that clears all active filters and restores the unfiltered list.

The table groups rows by each unique set of replacing requirements (e.g., if 10 requirements are all replaced by the same 2 requirements, they appear as a single grouped row. If 1 requirement is replaced by 1 other, it is its own row).

Table columns are: Checkbox (shown for Product Requirements only — Process Requirements are updated as a set), Change type, Requirement Name (with Old/New stamps for Updated/Replaced), Requirement Description (with Old/New stamps, collapsed to 2 lines with 'View more'), and Details ('View Details' link).

The 'Change' type values and their definitions are: 'Updated' when name/description/selection criteria differ in the latest version; 'Replaced' when the scoped version is Inactive and has replacing requirements in 'Is Replaced By'; 'Added' when a new or previously non-scoped requirement now qualifies (note: a replacement of a currently scoped requirement is 'Replaced', not 'Added'; a replacement of a currently not-scoped requirement is 'Added'); 'Removed' when a requirement is deactivated without replacement, its selection criteria no longer matches, or an Inactive requirement's replacements would not be scoped after re-scoping.

The Requirement Name column shows icons to distinguish requirement types: an open expand icon for parent requirements with sub-requirements in the list; a sub-requirement arrow icon for all sub-requirements; no icon for parent requirements without sub-requirements in the list. The list is sorted by Old requirement version order value, with parents displayed before their respective sub-requirements.
If a requirement in the list has a 'Change on date' value, that date is displayed in red at the end of the Requirement Name column entry for that row.

For 'Added' and 'Removed' changes, a single code+name and description are shown. For 'Updated' changes, two pairs are shown with 'Old' and 'New' stamps. For 'Replaced' changes, all old code+name pairs and all new code+name pairs are shown with stamps, supporting N-to-M replacement relationships.
Descriptions are collapsed to 2 lines by default with a 'View more' link at the end of the second line to expand the full text.
stories.source_requirement: Frontoffice section: 'Update the view and behavior of the Requirements Update pop-up' — including warning message variants, pop-up structure, filter definitions, table grouping logic, column descriptions, and Change type definitions.

stories.title: View Details of Individual Requirement Changes from the Requirements Update Pop-up
stories.as_a: release manager reviewing the Requirements Update pop-up
stories.i_want: to click 'View Details' on any change row and see a detailed comparison of the old and new requirement versions with all relevant status data
stories.so_that: I can make a fully informed decision about whether to accept or skip a specific requirement version change
acceptance_criteria: Clicking the 'View Details' link for an 'Added' change opens a details pop-up with: the requirement code and name in the header, an 'Added' stamp, and the full description of the added requirement.

Clicking 'View Details' for a 'Removed' change opens a pop-up with: the requirement code and name in the header, a 'Removed' stamp, the full description of the requirement, the current status of this requirement in the release, the Evidence Link (if one was added for this requirement in the release), and the Justification text (if one was added). If the status is 'Delegated' and 'Delegated To' details are available, a 'View Details' link is shown below the status — clicking it opens the 'Requirements Info' pop-up in a new browser window.

acceptance_criteria.text: Clicking 'View Details' for an 'Updated' change opens a pop-up with: the new requirement code and name in the header, an 'Updated' stamp, and two side-by-side columns. The left column (labelled 'Old') shows: requirement code and name, description, current status in the release, Evidence Link, Justification, and a 'View Details' link under the status if status is 'Delegated' with 'Delegated To' details available. The right column (labelled 'New') shows: the code and name from the latest version, and the description from the latest version. Fields that changed in the update are highlighted in green in the New column.

Clicking 'View Details' for a 'Replaced' change opens a pop-up with: a header in the format 'Replaced: <N> requirement/s → <M> requirement/s', a 'Replaced' stamp, and two columns. The left column (labelled 'Old') lists all replaced requirements (code+name, description, current status, Evidence Link, Justification, and Delegation 'View Details' link if applicable). The right column (labelled 'New') lists all replacing requirements (code+name, description). Multiple requirements in each column can be viewed by scrolling within the column.

acceptance_criteria: The details pop-up is strictly view-only — no fields are editable from this view. The pop-up can only be closed by clicking the 'Close' button or the X button.

When a 'View Details' delegation link is clicked from within the details pop-up, the 'Requirements Info' pop-up opens in a new browser window or tab, preserving the current details pop-up in its existing state.
stories.source_requirement: Frontoffice section: 'View details of the update on the Requirements Update pop-up' — detailing the pop-up structure for each change type: Added, Removed, Updated, and Replaced.

stories.title: Apply Selected Requirement Changes from the Requirements Update Pop-up
stories.as_a: release manager who has reviewed the Requirements Update pop-up and selected changes to apply
stories.i_want: to apply selected requirement changes to my release, with the system correctly adding, removing, updating, or replacing requirements and preserving existing status data where applicable
stories.so_that: only the changes I approve are incorporated into my release scope, with no loss of previously recorded evidence, justification, or delegation data

acceptance_criteria.text: When the user selects a change via checkbox (Product Requirements) and that change involves a parent requirement that also has sub-requirements in the change list — or a sub-requirement whose parent or sibling sub-requirements are also in the list (in both Old and New versions) — the system automatically pre-selects all related sub-requirements and the parent requirement. The informational message 'Related parent/sub-requirements were auto-selected. Review and deselect if needed.' is shown on the pop-up (replacing the existing yellow warning message if it was previously visible).

acceptance_criteria: After selecting all desired changes, the user clicks 'Apply Selected Changes'. If any selected changes are 'Updated' or 'Replaced' AND the current version of those requirements in the release is in any status other than 'Planned', OR if any 'Evidence Link' or 'Justification' fields are filled for those requirements — the changes are NOT immediately applied. Instead, the 'Update Requirements Status' page opens within the same pop-up.

If none of the selected changes are 'Updated' or 'Replaced', OR if all 'Updated'/'Replaced' requirements are in 'Planned' status with no 'Evidence Link' or 'Justification' filled — the changes are applied immediately to the release without opening the 'Update Requirements Status' page.

When changes are applied immediately: 'Added' requirements are added to the release scope; 'Removed' requirements are removed from scope; 'Updated' requirements have their version updated to the latest, with existing Jama/Jira linked tickets and Delegation details (Delegated From) preserved. 'Replaced' requirements are removed from the scope, and the requirements listed in their 'Is Replaced By' field are added to the scope. 'Delegated From' delegation details are transferred to the replacing requirements.

For Product Requirements replacements: Jama/Jira tickets linked to the old replaced version are NOT automatically transferred to the replacing requirement. The replacing requirement is added with the status from the old requirement, but relinking/submitting to Jama/Jira and removing no-longer-applicable tickets must be done manually by the user. The system must display an informational note to this effect after applying.

Process Requirements are updated as a complete set (no individual selection). The same transfer logic applies: for 1-to-1 replacements, Evidence, Justification, and Delegation details (Delegated From) are transferred. For many-to-1 replacements, Evidence, Justification, and Delegation details (Delegated From) are also transferred. Jama/Jira tickets are not transferred automatically in either case.
For all newly applied sub-requirement versions, their parent requirement is also added to the release scope, even if the parent's change was not explicitly selected by the user.

Clicking 'Keep Current Version' or the X button on the 'Requirements Update' pop-up: no changes are applied. The current requirement versions remain in the release unchanged, even if the Questionnaire, Risk Classification, or Data Protection & Privacy Risk is subsequently updated. Only changes with a 'Change on date' that has passed will still be automatically applied on the scheduled date regardless of this choice.
stories.source_requirement: Frontoffice section: 'Actions on the Requirements Update pop-up' — Product Requirements and Process Requirements sub-sections, covering checkbox auto-selection, conditional navigation to Update Requirements Status, immediate apply logic, field transfer rules, and Keep Current Version behavior.

stories.title: Configure, Navigate, and Commit Requirement Statuses on the 'Update Requirements Status' Page
stories.as_a: release manager whose selected changes include Updated or Replaced requirements with non-Planned statuses
stories.i_want: to review old requirement statuses, configure statuses for new requirement versions, navigate back to adjust my selection, and finally commit or cancel the full update
stories.so_that: previously recorded evidence, justification, and delegation data is correctly carried forward and I retain full control over what is applied to my release

acceptance_criteria.text: The 'Update Requirements Status' page shows sub-header: 'Review the requirements and set their statuses in the New status column.' and, if applicable, the warning 'Certain requirements will be updated automatically on the specified date.' (same condition as the main pop-up).

The page displays a table grouped by each unique New Requirement Name. Table columns: Checkbox, Old Requirement Name (showing all relevant old codes+names; 'Change on date' shown in red if applicable), Old Status (current status of each old requirement in the release), New Requirement Name (one new requirement per row), New Status (pre-filled by transfer rules below), and Actions (Edit icon).

acceptance_criteria: Default status transfer rules applied on page load: (1) 1-to-1 update or 1-to-1 replacement where old status is 'In Progress', 'Partial', or 'Postponed' — status is transferred together with Evidence Link, Justification, and Delegation details (Delegated From). For 'Updated' changes, Jama/Jira details are also transferred. (2) 1-to-1 update or 1-to-1 replacement where old status is 'Not Applicable', 'Done', or 'Delegated' — status is NOT transferred (New Status = Planned), but Evidence Link, Justification, and Delegation details (both Delegated To and Delegated From) are transferred. For 'Updated' changes, Jama/Jira details are also transferred. (3) Many-to-1 or 1-to-many — New Status = Planned, Evidence Link and Justification are empty; no transfer occurs.

acceptance_criteria.text: The user can edit the New Status for an individual requirement by clicking the 'Edit' icon in the Actions column or the 3-dots button. This opens an 'Edit Requirement' pop-up. Left side (scrollable): Change type stamp, and for each old requirement: Requirement name, Description, Status, Evidence Link, Justification, Source Status (if available), Backlog Link (if available). Right side: 'New' stamp and editable fields — Requirement name, Description, Status (dropdown), Evidence Link (mandatory if status is 'Done' or 'Partial'), Justification (mandatory if status is 'Not Applicable', 'Postponed', 'Partial', or 'Delegated'). When 'Delegated' is selected, delegation fields expand. Fields changed in an 'Updated' requirement are highlighted in green in the right column. Any pre-transferred data is pre-filled in the corresponding right-side fields.

acceptance_criteria: 'Save Changes' in the Edit pop-up validates mandatory fields. If valid, saves the configured data for that row on the Status page and closes the pop-up; the 'Update Requirements Status' pop-up remains open. If invalid, inline errors are shown under the failing fields and the pop-up remains open. 'Cancel' or X closes the pop-up without saving.

acceptance_criteria.text: The user can select multiple requirements using checkboxes and click the 'Edit' bulk action button, opening the 'Edit Selected Requirements' pop-up. This pop-up shows each selected change as a collapsible section (header, old details, new details). A sticky bottom section holds the shared editable fields (Status, Evidence Link, Justification) with the same mandatory rules. The sticky bottom section has its own scroll if content overflows. 'Save Changes' applies the same values to all selected requirements.

The user can select requirements and click 'Transfer Requirement Statuses'. A 'Confirm Status Transfer' pop-up is shown. If 'Confirm' is clicked: for Updated requirements — status + Evidence + Justification + Delegation (Delegated From and To) + Jama/Jira + all integration fields are transferred. For 1-to-1 replacements — status + Evidence + Justification + Delegation (Delegated From and To) are transferred; Jama/Jira are not. For many-to-1 replacements — nothing is transferred (must be done manually by the user). The 'Transfer Requirement Statuses' button is disabled if only many-to-1 changes are selected. If 'Cancel' or X is clicked, no transfer occurs.

acceptance_criteria: Clicking the back arrow in the header navigates back to the main Requirements Update change list. All previously selected checkboxes on the change list are preserved. All statuses already configured on the 'Update Requirements Status' page are also preserved. To return to the 'Update Requirements Status' page, the user clicks 'Apply Selected Changes' again.

Clicking 'Save Changes' on the 'Update Requirements Status' page commits all changes to the release: Added requirements added to scope; Removed requirements removed; Updated requirements updated to latest version (requirements not shown on the Status page keep their existing Evidence, Justification, Jama/Jira, and Delegation (Delegated From); those shown use the configured/transferred data); Replaced requirements removed and their 'Is Replaced By' replacements added (same transfer logic). Sub-requirements' parent requirements are always added to scope even if not explicitly selected. Jama/Jira from replaced requirements are not auto-transferred.

Clicking 'Cancel' or X on the 'Update Requirements Status' page discards all pending changes. All current requirement versions in the release remain unchanged. Only mandatory changes with a 'Change on date' will still be applied automatically on the scheduled date.
stories.source_requirement: Frontoffice sections: ''Update Requirements Status' page view and details', 'Edit requirements status on Update Requirements Status page', 'Actions on the Update Requirements Status page and navigations'.

stories.title: Preserve Requirement Versions Across Questionnaire, Risk, and New Release Scenarios
stories.as_a: release manager working with requirements in a release that has already been scoped
stories.i_want: the system to preserve the requirement versions I have in my release when the questionnaire or risk is updated, and to always start new releases from the correct version baseline
stories.so_that: requirement version changes never apply silently without my explicit approval and I always know which version baseline applies to a given release
acceptance_criteria: Without the user confirming changes on the 'Requirements Update' pop-up, updating the Questionnaire, Risk Classification, or Data Protection & Privacy Risk in a release does NOT apply the latest library requirement versions. Re-scoping after such updates uses the set of requirement versions already present in the release, not the latest library versions.

When a brand-new release is created from scratch and the questionnaire has not yet been completed: upon questionnaire completion, the latest versions of all requirements are scoped for the release and saved as the product's latest applicable set. No version change pop-up is shown. Inactive requirements are not shown in the requirement list.

For an existing product where no release has been scoped (e.g., all previous releases were cancelled before questionnaire completion or none existed): same behavior as a new product — latest versions are used on questionnaire completion, and no version change pop-up is shown.

When a release that was past the FCSR Readiness Sign-off stage is returned to 'Manage' stage: the requirements version change pop-up is shown after the stage change. Mandatory updates (those with a 'Change on date') are NOT automatically added for this case; each change must be selected and accepted individually by the user.

When a release is created as a clone of an existing release: all requirements (scoped and not scoped) are copied with the versions available in the original release at the time of cloning. The version change pop-up is shown in the cloned release immediately. Mandatory updates are not automatically added. After questionnaire or risk changes in the cloned release, re-scoping uses the version set transferred from the original release — not the latest library versions.

acceptance_criteria.text: For an existing release in 'Scoping' stage when Backoffice requirements are updated: the user sees the version change pop-up. Mandatory updates with 'Change on date' are automatically applied to this release and shown on the pop-up with an 'already added' indicator. If only partial or no changes are accepted, subsequent questionnaire re-completion re-scopes using the version set currently in the release.

acceptance_criteria: For an existing release in 'Review & Confirm' stage when Backoffice requirements are updated: same behavior as Scoping — pop-up shown, mandatory updates auto-applied. If partial/no changes are accepted and risks are subsequently updated, re-scoping uses current release versions.
For an existing release in 'Manage' stage when Backoffice requirements are updated: the version change pop-up is shown, but mandatory updates are NOT automatically applied to this release.
For releases in final statuses (Cancelled, Inactive, Completed) or past the FCSR Readiness Sign-off stage: no version change pop-up is shown and requirements cannot be updated in these releases.

A new release created from scratch for an existing product that also has active releases with scoped requirements: the new release still uses the latest library versions upon questionnaire completion, with no version change pop-up. Inactive requirements are excluded from the requirement list.
stories.source_requirement: Frontoffice section: 'Latest version of requirements should not be added after risk or questionnaire update' and the 'Main use cases and expected behaviour' table covering: New product, Existing product (no releases), Existing product (no active releases), Existing product (active releases) — across Scoping, Review & Confirm, and Manage stages.

stories.title: Handle Mandatory Scheduled Requirement Updates and Post-Update Notification Pop-up
stories.as_a: release manager whose release received mandatory requirement changes automatically applied on a scheduled date
stories.i_want: to see a notification with a read-only pop-up listing all the changes that were automatically applied to my release
stories.so_that: I am fully informed of what was changed in my release without any action on my part and can review the details of each automatic update
acceptance_criteria: When mandatory requirement updates (those with a 'Change on date' specified in Backoffice) are automatically applied to a release on the scheduled date, a notification is shown on releases in 'Creation & Scoping' and 'Review & Confirm' stages. The notification includes a 'Learn More' button.
Clicking 'Learn More' opens a post-update 'Requirements Update' pop-up. This pop-up is read-only and shows the changes that have already been applied.

acceptance_criteria.text: The post-update pop-up displays: a clickable 'Link to the Content Changes', sub-header: 'The following requirements scoped for this release were updated.', and filter controls: Search (by requirement code, name, or description), a 'Change' dropdown (options: Updated, Replaced, Added, Removed), and a 'Reset' button.

acceptance_criteria: The table structure in the post-update pop-up mirrors the proactive Requirements Update pop-up: rows are grouped by each unique set of replacing requirements; columns include Change type, Requirement Name (with Old/New stamps for Updated and Replaced), Requirement Description (with Old/New stamps, collapsed to 2 lines with 'View more'), and a 'View Details' link. There are no checkboxes — all changes are already applied.
If a requirement in the list has a 'Change on date' value, that date is shown in red at the end of the Requirement Name column entry.

Mandatory changes with a 'Change on date' are applied automatically on the scheduled date even if the user previously clicked 'Keep Current Version' on the proactive 'Requirements Update' pop-up for those requirements.
Clicking 'View Details' on any row in the post-update pop-up opens the same type-specific detail view as the proactive pop-up (Added, Removed, Updated, Replaced views — per the 'View Details' story).
stories.source_requirement: Frontoffice section: 'Already updated requirements pop-up update' — notification trigger, pop-up structure, sub-header text, filter controls, table definition, and mandatory change override behavior.

stories.title: Update Not-Scoped Requirements to the Latest Version After Confirmation or Re-scoping
stories.as_a: release manager managing requirement scope in my release
stories.i_want: not-scoped requirements to be automatically updated to the latest library version when I confirm version changes or complete re-scoping, and to be prompted to switch to the latest version when I manually add a requirement that has a newer version available
stories.so_that: the full requirement library in my release (including not-scoped entries) stays current and I never unknowingly add an outdated requirement manually
acceptance_criteria: When the user confirms requirement version changes on the 'Requirements Update' pop-up (by clicking 'Apply Selected Changes' or 'Yes, change the version'), any not-scoped requirements in the release are automatically updated to the latest version available in the library. This update occurs in the background and does not require additional user action.

When re-scoping is completed (e.g., after questionnaire completion or a risk classification change), not-scoped requirements in the release are also updated to the latest library version as part of the re-scoping process.

If the user chooses to keep the current version for scoped requirements (via 'Keep Current Version' on the pop-up), not-scoped requirements in the release still retain the version that was current at the time of that choice. They are not updated to the latest library version in this case.
This version preservation behavior also applies to cloned releases: not-scoped requirements in a clone reflect the versions from the original release, not the latest library versions.

When the user manually adds a requirement to the release that is currently not in scope and a newer version of that requirement exists in the library, the system displays a version change pop-up for that requirement. The pop-up allows the user to choose between keeping the version currently in the release or switching to the latest library version.
Inactive requirements are never shown in the list of all requirements and are automatically removed from the requirement list after any re-scoping operation.
stories.source_requirement: Frontoffice section: 'Impact of the requirements update for not scoped requirements' — covering automatic version update on confirmation, behavior when current version is kept, clone behavior, and manual add prompt.

stories.title: Show Questionnaire Warning for New Unanswered Questions and Allow Questionnaire Edit
stories.as_a: release manager in Scoping, Scope Approval, or Manage stage
stories.i_want: to see a warning message when new unanswered questionnaire questions have been added after I completed the questionnaire, and to be allowed to edit the questionnaire to answer them
stories.so_that: my release scope is always based on a complete and up-to-date questionnaire and I never miss new mandatory questions introduced after my initial completion
acceptance_criteria: When any unanswered question is present on the Questionnaire tab after the questionnaire was previously completed (i.e., new questions have been added to the questionnaire library after completion), a warning message is displayed on the Questionnaire tab for all releases in 'Scoping', 'Scope Approval', and 'Manage' stages.

acceptance_criteria.text: The warning message format varies depending on whether a requirements version change notification is also active (due to Major requirement changes): if both are active and the release was created from scratch, both notifications are combined in a single message; if both are active and the release was cloned, a different combined format is used. The exact message text for each variant must be confirmed with the design/product team and is shown in the Confluence spec screenshots.

Users who have the privilege to edit the questionnaire in a release are allowed to edit it during 'Scope Approval' and 'Manage' stages — but only when new unanswered questions are present. If no new unanswered questions exist, the questionnaire remains non-editable at these stages regardless of other conditions.

acceptance_criteria: If no new unanswered questions are present but a Major requirements version change is active, only the requirements version change notification is shown on the Questionnaire tab. No questionnaire edit permission is granted based solely on a version change.
stories.source_requirement: Frontoffice section: 'Update questionnaire behavior' — covering warning messages on Questionnaire tab, combined warnings for releases from scratch and cloned releases, and privilege-based questionnaire edit during Scope Approval and Manage stages.

stories.title: Update Tableau 'Actual on Date' Reports to Filter Requirements by Release
stories.as_a: product compliance officer using Tableau reports to review requirement statuses
stories.i_want: the Product Requirements and Process Requirements 'Actual on Date' reports to support filtering by both product and a specific associated release, showing only requirements linked to that release ID
stories.so_that: I can generate accurate release-specific compliance reports instead of viewing requirements for the entire product

acceptance_criteria.text: The 'Product Requirements Actual on Date' Tableau report's Product/Release filter allows the user to first select a product and then select a specific release associated with that product from a subordinate dropdown. When a release is selected, the report shows only requirements associated with that specific release ID.

acceptance_criteria: The 'Process Requirements Actual on Date' Tableau report's Product/Release filter has the same behavior: product selection followed by optional release selection, with the report filtered to the selected release ID when a release is chosen.
The release dropdown in the filter shows only releases belonging to the currently selected product. Releases from other products are not shown.
Selecting a product without choosing a specific release is still supported. In this case, the report displays data at the product level, unchanged from the current behavior.
When a release is selected and then the product selection is changed, the release dropdown resets to 'no release selected' to prevent cross-product release selections.

If a release is selected and the report is filtered to that release ID, requirements that are not associated with the selected release (even if they belong to the same product) are excluded from the report results.
stories.source_requirement: Tableau reports update section: 'Product/Release filter behaviour should be changed: It should still allow to select the product and release related to it. By using this filter the user can see requirements related to this release (associated with specific release id).'

questions.title: Questionnaire warning message exact text not specified — screenshots only
questions.detail: Story 11 (Show Questionnaire Warning): The spec shows the combined warning message formats (for from-scratch vs. cloned releases when both questionnaire and version change warnings are active) as screenshots only — the exact copy text is not written out in the spec. The acceptance criteria reference those screenshots but the final message strings must be confirmed and provided by the product/design team before implementation.

questions.title: PIC-10186 marked as 'In question?' — scope needs confirmation
questions.detail: The spec's story table marks the JIRA ticket PIC-10186 with 'In question?' — it is unclear whether this story is confirmed for development in the current release. The team must confirm whether PIC-10186 is in scope before it is groomed and scheduled.

questions.title: CTI/CTS requirements mapping behavior is unresolved
questions.detail: The spec's Q&A section (Questions 5–6) raises complex open questions about CTI/CTS requirements: how they map to IEC62443 and OWASP/CCM standards, whether completing a CTI/CTS requirement marks the linked standard requirement as complete, how partial CTI/CTS applicability affects standard requirements, and how CTI/CTS grouping should be displayed in the UI. None of these have definitive answers in the spec. A separate specification or design decision document is needed before CTI/CTS-related behavior can be implemented.

questions.title: Status transfer for many-to-many replacements is undefined
questions.detail: Story 7 (Configure Statuses on Update Requirements Status page): The spec defines transfer rules for 1-to-1 and many-to-1 replacements but does not address many-to-many (e.g., 3 requirements replaced by 2). Current stories treat this as 'no transfer' (consistent with the many-to-1 rule), but this needs explicit confirmation from the product team to avoid incorrect implementation.

questions.title: Stories 7 and 11 flagged in spec as possibly needing to be split or combined
questions.detail: The spec itself annotates 'Edit requirements status on Update Requirements Status page' with '(Maybe would be splitted)' and 'Impact of the requirements update for not scoped requirements' with '(Maybe would be combined with other)'. These have been written as independent stories (Stories 7 and 10 in this output) but the team should validate the story boundaries during grooming and split or merge as needed.
